resources.string: BCO needs Bluetooth access to manage your headset. The permission was removed — grant it again so BCO can reconnect.
Something went wrong. Please try again.
No headset is selected. Choose a device in BCO settings first.
The saved headset address is invalid. Pick your headset again in settings.
The saved headset is not paired with this phone. Pair it in system Bluetooth settings.
BCO could not start the Bluetooth handover. Try starting the service again.
Turn on Bluetooth and try again.
BCO needs Bluetooth permission to connect the headset. Grant it in system settings.
The system did not connect the headset. Check Bluetooth and try again.
The headset did not disconnect in time to run a fresh reconnect. Try again.
Bluetooth action failed: %1$s. If this keeps happening, pick your headset again in settings.
Test connect succeeded. The system accepted the connection request.
Engine is not ready. Try starting the service again.
Automatic Bluetooth switching is not supported on this device. You may need to switch manually.
Keeps headset coordination running in the background
Sound and heads-up alerts when another device asks to pair with BCO
Silent pairing requests you can review from the notification shade
%1$s wants to pair · confirm code %2$s matches on both devices
%1$s wants to pair · headset: %2$s · confirm code %3$s matches on both devices
Native core not included in this build

This APK was built without libbconet.so, so BCO cannot start the mesh engine.

Developers: install the Android NDK (SDK Manager), then from bco-core run:

 ANDROID_NDK_HOME=$ANDROID_HOME/ndk/VERSION make android-arm64

Copy the library:

 cp build/android-arm64/libbconet.so ../bco-shell/androidApp/src/main/jniLibs/arm64-v8a/

Rebuild and reinstall the app. See bco-core/README.md and bco-shell/androidApp/src/main/jniLibs/arm64-v8a/README.md.
